qa_pairs.question: What type of error can occur when installing packages in a Jupyter notebook if `pip` attempts to access a read-only location?
qa_pairs.answer: When installing packages in a Jupyter notebook, users may encounter an "ERROR: Could not install packages due to an OSError: [Errno 30] Read-only file system" if `pip` tries to uninstall a package from a read-only directory.

qa_pairs.question: How can one attempt to resolve the "Read-only file system" error during package installation in a Jupyter notebook, and what are the limitations of this solution?
qa_pairs.answer: To address the "Read-only file system" error when installing packages in a Jupyter notebook, users can try using `pip install --no-index --ignore-installed <package>` in a notebook cell. However, this workaround may not be effective for all packages, such as `pyarrow`, `opencv`, or `mpi4py`.
